summary: The author secured 2nd place by focusing on stage 2 segmentation and post-processing for ECG image digitization. Key innovations included replacing competition data with original 500Hz PTB-XL signals to ensure consistent sampling, designing sparse masks with fractional y-coordinate distribution for sub-pixel precision, and building a 2.5D series model alongside a whole-image model. The pipeline leveraged timm encoders with a custom decoder, fused multi-lead features via conv2d, and optimized post-processing with weighted averaging and scipy resampling to maximize SNR.
extensive_summary:
## Data Strategy & Mask Creation
The solution builds on @hengck23's baseline for stages 0 and 1, focusing primarily on stage 2 segmentation. A major data insight was replacing the competition's resampled signals with the original 500Hz PTB-XL time-series data, which standardized sampling positions and improved CV/LB scores. To handle potential private-set difficulties, a small amount of synthetic `0015` wrinkled data was injected per epoch. Crucially, the author abandoned dense masks in favor of sparse masks (max 2 pixels per column), distributing labels to integer and integer+1 pixels based on fractional y-coordinates. This enabled sub-pixel reconstruction via weighted averaging during post-processing.

## Model Architectures
Two complementary models were developed. The **whole model** uses timm encoders paired with @hengck23's `MyCoordUnetDecoder`, cropping the top image portion and resizing to (1280, 5600). The **series model** is a 2.5D architecture that takes four ECG series as input, crops each to a ±3mV (240px) range around zero, and processes them through a shared U-Net encoder. Features are fused across leads in the decoder paths using three tested modules: conv2d, shared conv2d, and conv3d. The conv2d fusion module proved most effective for mixing depth information and recovering masked/artifacted regions by leveraging inter-lead correlations.

## Training & Post-Processing
Training utilized BCEWithLogitsLoss(pos_weight=20), AdamW, CosineAnnealingLR, and gradient checkpointing for memory efficiency. Augmentations included brightness/contrast shifts, shadows, Gaussian blur, coarse dropout, grayscale conversion, and horizontal flips for masks. During inference, horizontal flip TTA was applied. Post-processing converted mask predictions to time-series data by computing weighted y-coordinate averages per column, followed by `scipy.signal.resample`, which outperformed alternative resampling methods. Final submissions ensembled 6 to 11 models across various EfficientNet/EfficientNetV2 encoders and architectures.
competition_flow: Raw ECG images are processed through a baseline stage 0/1 pipeline, then fed into sparse mask segmentation models (whole and 2.5D series) to predict pixel-wise signal positions, followed by weighted averaging and scipy resampling to reconstruct time-series data for SNR-based evaluation.
data_processing: Replaced competition data with original 500Hz PTB-XL signals
Added small amount of synthetic `0015` wrinkled data per epoch
Created sparse masks (max 2 pixels/column) with fractional y-coordinate distribution
Applied image augmentations (brightness/contrast, shadows, blur, dropout, grayscale) and mask augmentations (horizontal flip)
Used gradient checkpointing for memory optimization
models: timm encoders (EfficientNet B7, EfficientNetV2 L, EfficientNetV2 M, EfficientNet B6, EfficientNet B4)
ensembling: Ensembled 6 to 11 models using various EfficientNet/EfficientNetV2 encoders with whole or series architectures, applied horizontal flip TTA, and used weighted averaging with scipy resampling for mask-to-series conversion.
insights: Pushing medium-to-high SNR images higher improves the final score more than focusing on low-SNR cases.
Sparse masks with fractional y-coordinate distribution enable sub-pixel precision and higher reconstruction SNR than dense masks.
Fusing features across ECG leads via conv2d effectively shares phase and amplitude information, improving predictions on masked/artifacted regions.
Gradient checkpointing is highly effective for reducing activation memory consumption with high-resolution images.
critical_findings: Replacing competition data with original 500Hz PTB-XL signals improved consistency in sampling positions and boosted LB score by ~0.8dB.
Dense masks covering the entire signal line did not yield high reconstruction SNR, whereas sparse masks annotated at most 2 pixels per column performed significantly better.
LSTM fusion modules performed poorly, likely due to parameter settings, while conv2d outperformed conv3d for mixing depth information in the 2.5D model.
what_did_not_work: LSTM fusion modules performed poorly (possibly due to poor parameter settings)
Synthetic data provided only marginal improvements beyond competition data alone

papers.title: ECG-Image-Kit: a synthetic image generation toolbox to facilitate deep learning-based electrocardiogram digitization
ECG-Image-Database: A dataset of ECG images with real-world imaging and scanning artifacts; a foundation for computerized ECG image digitization and analysis
PTB-XL, a large publicly available electrocardiography dataset (version 1.0.3)
PTB-XL: A Large Publicly Available ECG Dataset
PhysioBank, PhysioToolkit, and PhysioNet: Components of a new research resource for complex physiologic signals
